questions.questionText: One of the few sports that originated in ______________ is the game volleyball.

Volleyball was invented in ____ by William G. Morgan, a YMCA director in Holyoke, Massachussets, which he initially called mintonette in attempt to meet the needs of local businessmen who found the game basketball to be strenuous.

Volleyball was invented in 1895 by _____________, a YMCA director in Holyoke, Massachussets, which he initially called mintonette in attempt to meet the needs of local businessmen who found the game basketball to be strenuous.

Volleyball was invented in 1895 by William G. Morgan, a ___________________ in Holyoke, Massachussets, which he initially called mintonette in attempt to meet the needs of local businessmen who found the game basketball to be strenuous.

Volleyball was invented in 1895 by William G. Morgan, a YMCA director in Holyoke, Massachussets, which he initially called ____________ in attempt to meet the needs of local businessmen who found the game basketball to be strenuous.

Volleyball was invented in 1895 by William G. Morgan, a YMCA director in Holyoke, Massachussets, which he initially called mintonette in attempt to meet the needs of ________________ who found the game basketball to be __________.
____________________ who after viewing a game, felt that volleyball would be a more suitable name due to the volleying characteristics of play (Viera & Ferguson, 1989).
4 Parts of the ball.

The game was introduced to the Philippines in 1910 by the Physical Director of the YMCA, ______________, to Japan in 1913, to Poland in 1915, to Uruguay in 1916, to Brazil and Latvia in 1912, and to Syria in 1922. After World War I, it was introduced throught Europe.

The japanese added the sport to the Olympic Games program in ____. These contributed to the fast growth of volleyball in the last 3 decades.
The _________ added the sport to the _________________ program in 1964. These contributed to the fast growth of volleyball in the last 3 decades.
A complex game of simple skills, and is usually played indoors. There is no time limit on a game, but there are Sets.
A player is allowed to hit the ball twice in a row. (True or False).
The only exception a player is allowed to hit a ball twice in a row is tempting a block. (True or False).
Volleyball Court is 18 meters long and 9 meters wide. (True or False).
Volleyball Court is 18 meters wide and 9 meters long. (True or False).
Volleyball Court is __ meters long and __ meters wide.
The net is higher on the girl's court. (True or False).
The net is higher on the boy's court. (True or False).

This ball of volleyball is the official balls for the US national teams and the youth teams since 1997 and 2001, respectively. This is also the official ball of the NCAA me's division and used by many different collegiate conference games.
This ball of volleyball are the official ones used by the FIVB in worldwide competitions. It is also one of the top indoor balls available and has become the ball of choice for all nations.
The net streteched across the court, __ meter (__ feet) deep and ___ meters (__ feet __ inches long). The net height is 2.43 meters for men, and 2.24 meters high for women.
In Volleyball, the net height is ______ meters for men, and ______ meters high for women.
In volleyball, how many players for each of the zone?
What are the 2 zones in Volleyball?
They are the players who are allowed to block the opponent and attack the ball in the attack zone.
They are the players who play by defense by digging opponent's attacks and attack the ball behind attack line (3 meter line, 10 feet line).
Players are rotating __________________ on the court after winning the _____ after the opponent's _______.
They are also known as the wing spiker, and left spiker. They carries ther serve receive responsibility along with the Libero.

They are also known as the wing spiker, and right spiker. They are responsible for blocking the opponent's outside hitter, which means the person who plays opposite needs to be a solid blocker as well as good hitter.
They are the playmaker, the backbone of the offense and makes the decisions about who gets the ball when. She touches the ball on the 2nd contact and delivers it to her hitters.
Their main responsibility is to stop opponent's offense. They build a block which stops the ball, or allows the team to dig the ball up.

They play in the back row and has impeccable ball control. They need to be a great passer and an even better digger. She is all over the court to keep the ball in the air for her team to create scoring chances.

name: PATHFIT4 Topic 3 - Volleyball Officials and Skills

questions.questionText: The _____________ sits in a high chair on a level with the top of the net, so he has a clear view across the top of the net and the court. A referee's duties include signaling when a really begins and ends. The referee is responsible for officially recognizing team requests, substitutions, time-outs and communicating with the coaches at the appropriate times.
The _______________ stands opposite the first referee, close to the scorer's table and substitute benches. They say whether the ball is in or out, whether a player touches the ball. (Linesmen).

The ___________ creates official records of volleyball games. Before play begins, he records team and player information. Once the game begins, he tracks points, substitutions, sanctions, and time-outs. He also tracks the rotation and notify referees of potential lapses.

The _____ is a forearm pass that is used to control the ball and pass it to the setter at the net. It is usually the first contact by the team and an effective shot to use in defense, such as when receiving a spike. The libero handles much of the team's serve reception and is puvotal in backcourt defense.
The _____ is an overhead pass used to change the direction of the dig and put the ball in a good position for the spiker. It is usually the team's second contact to the ball.
The ______ is when the ball is hit or smashed across the net. It is the most powerful shot in volleyball and the most effective way to win a rally.
This is the first line of defense in volleyball. The objective is to stop the spiked ball from crossing the net or to channel the spike to defenders.

name: PATHFIT4 Topic 4 - Volleyball Referee Signals and Rule Violations

questions.questionText: Volleyball violation where players are not in the correct rotation order when the ball is served, or the wrong player serves.
Volleyball violation where a player steps on or over a boundary line illegally (commonly the service line before hitting the serve).
Volleyball violation where a player contacts the ball in an improper way (e.g., catching, throwing, lifting, or prolonged contact).
Volleyball violation where the server takes too long to serve after the referee signals to begin.
Volleyball violation where a player reaches over the net illegally to play the ball (usually interfering with the opponent’s play).
Volleyball violation where a player touches the net during play, or a served ball hit the net in a way that causes a fault (depending on rules used).
Volleyball hand signal where a back-row player attacks the ball legally (e.g., jumps from behind the attack line).
Volleyball violation where a back-row player attacks the ball illegally (like jumping in front of the attack line), or a player attacks a served ball improperly.
questions.answer: Illegal Attack of Server / Back Row Attack

questions.questionText: Volleyball violation where a blocking is done illegally (like a back-row player blocking), or teammates block the server’s view of the ball (screening).
Volleyball hand signal where the ball touched a player before going out of bounds, so the point goes to the opponent.
Volleyball violation where a team hits the ball more than three times before sending it over the net (not counting a block touch).
Volleyball violation where a player hits the ball twice in succession (except during a legal first team contact like a dig).
Volleyball hand signal where the ball lands inside the court lines—point is awarded to the attacking team.
Volleyball violation where the ball lands outside the boundary lines or crosses the net outside the antenna.
Volleyball hand signal where the referee signals the server may serve the ball.
Volleyball hand signal where the referee allows a substitute or player to enter the court.
Volleyball hand signal where the referee indicates which team won the rally and gets the point.
Volleyball hand signal where the rally is replayed (for example, due to interference or simultaneous faults).
Volleyball hand signal where a legal player substitution is allowed.
Volleyball hand signal where a substitution breaks the rules (wrong player, too many substitutions, etc.).
Volleyball hand signal where a regular time-out requested by a team.
Volleyball hand signal of a stoppage called by referees (injury, equipment issue, etc.).
Volleyball hand signal where a team intentionally or repeatedly delays the game (warning or penalty may follow).
Volleyball hand signal when the match or set is finished.
Volleyball hand signal where the teams switch sides of the court (usually between sets or at certain scores).
